3 min read
4 hours ago

--

The way we build software has changed dramatically.
A few years ago, writing a React component meant opening your editor and building everything from scratch.
Today, you can ask an AI assistant to generate an entire UI component in seconds.
Tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, Bolt, and Lovable have made frontend development faster than ever.
But while code generation has become almost effortless, one part of the workflow still feels outdated:
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Previewing the result.
The Unexpected Bottleneck
Whenever I generated a JSX component with AI, I found myself repeating the same routine.

• Create a temporary React project

• Copy the JSX file

• Install dependencies

• Resolve missing imports

• Start the development server

• Wait for everything to compile
Only then could I answer a simple question:
“Does this component look the way I expected?”
After doing this dozens of times, I realized the problem wasn’t React.
The problem was the workflow.
Why Isn’t a JSX File Easy to Open?
Think about it.
If someone sends you an image, you double-click it.
If someone sends you a PDF, you open it instantly.
If someone sends you a video, it plays immediately.
So why should a JSX file require creating an entire project before you can even see it?
That question became the inspiration behind PreviewKit.
Building PreviewKit
I wanted a tool that could make JSX previewing feel natural.
No unnecessary setup.
No temporary projects.
No switching between multiple applications.
Just open the file and preview the component.
PreviewKit is a lightweight Windows application designed specifically for this purpose.
Whether the JSX file comes from an AI assistant or your own codebase, the experience should be fast and straightforward.
Designed for Modern Developers
Today’s developers aren’t just writing code.
They’re reviewing AI-generated components, experimenting with different UI ideas, and iterating faster than ever before.
That means visual feedback needs to be just as fast as code generation.
PreviewKit helps shorten the gap between receiving a JSX file and seeing the final result.
Instead of spending time preparing a development environment, you can immediately focus on evaluating the interface.
A Small Tool That Solves a Real Problem
Not every developer tool needs hundreds of features.
Sometimes solving one problem really well is enough.
PreviewKit isn’t a replacement for React, Vite, or Next.js.
It’s simply a faster way to answer one important question:
“What does this component look like?”
For developers who preview dozens of JSX files every week, removing even a minute from that process can have a meaningful impact.
Looking Ahead
As AI continues to accelerate software development, the surrounding tools need to evolve too.
Generating code is no longer the challenge.
Reviewing and validating that code efficiently is becoming just as important.
PreviewKit was built with that future in mind.
A simple tool for a simple purpose:
Open JSX files instantly, preview React components effortlessly, and spend more time building instead of setting up.
Because the best development workflow is the one that gets out of your way.
